stories.title: Open Championship R3 — Lucas Herbert Leads at -10 After Record-Tying 62 in R2

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Lucas Herbert fired a 62 in Round 2 at Royal Birkdale.

That ties the all-time men's major scoring record for a single round.

He leads The Open at -10. He plays for LIV Golf.

Moving day starts NOW. 👏

#TheOpen #BritishOpen #LIVGolf

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
54-hole Open Championship leaderboard entering Sunday:

🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿 Herbert (LIV) — leads
-8: Suber / Young / Gerard
-7: Burns
-5: DeChambeau (LIV)
-4: Scheffler / Fleetwood / Rahm (LIV)
-1: McIlroy

What a Sunday is coming. 🏌️

#TheOpen #BritishOpen

article.headline: LIV's Lucas Herbert Fires Record-Tying 62, Leads Open Championship at -10

research.facts:
Lucas Herbert Leads Open Championship at -10
**Sources:** Yahoo Sports (Herbert profile), Olympics.com (leaderboard), Sky Sports, PGA TOUR
**Verified facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Lucas Herbert plays for Ripper GC on the LIV Golf circuit
- Herbert shot 62 in R2 — matching the men's major championship scoring record for a single round
- He birdied his first three holes in R2; went out in 28 on the front nine
- After 36 holes: Herbert -10, leading the field
- He won LIV Golf Virginia in May 2026 — earned his Open spot via LIV
- He has four other top-10s in the 2026 LIV season
- He is 30 years old, Australian
- R3 final group: Herbert (-10) + Jackson Suber (-8)
- T2 at -8: Jackson Suber, Cameron Young, Ryan Gerard
- Sam Burns also shot 62 in R2 and is at -7
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- Scheffler entered R1 with -2 after R1; -4 after 36 holes (consistent with multiple reports showing -4 total, 2 rounds of 68)

stories.title: DeChambeau Two-Stroke Penalty Controversy — Shot 66 but Penalized for Improving Swing Area

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Bryson DeChambeau shot 66 at the Open.

Then the R&A hit him with a 2-stroke penalty — Rule 8.1a — for stomping tall grass near his ball.

He was FURIOUS. Almost withdrew.

Then he confirmed he'd play. And sources say he's charging today. 👀

#TheOpen #DeChambeau #BritishOpen

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
The R&A on Bryson's penalty: "Intent is irrelevant under Rule 8.1a."

DeChambeau's camp: "He may withdraw."

DeChambeau at midnight: "I'm playing."

Now he's apparently charging through R3 at Royal Birkdale.

Spicy Saturday at The Open. 🌶️ #TheOpen #BritishOpen

article.headline: DeChambeau Shot 66. The R&A Took Two Strokes Back. Then He Said He Might Withdraw. Then He Showed Up.

research.facts:
DeChambeau Penalty Controversy
**Sources:** Golf Digest (penalty explained, Rule 8.1a), Yahoo Sports (DeChambeau responds), Yahoo Sports R3 live blog
**Verified facts (HIGH confidence):**
- DeChambeau shot 66 in R2
- He briefly reached -7 during/after R2 (solo second)
- R&A issued a 2-stroke post-round penalty under Rule 8.1a — "inadvertently improving the area of his intended swing" by stomping down tall grass near his ball on the 5th hole
- R&A chief referee Grant Moir issued the penalty; explicitly stated intent is irrelevant under the rule
- DeChambeau was "furious" per reports; his agent suggested possible withdrawal
- DeChambeau confirmed around midnight he would play R3
- After penalty, he enters R3 at -5 — five back of Herbert
- Yahoo Sports R3 live blog headline: "Can anyone fend off Bryson DeChambeau after his chaotic penalty?" — suggesting he is mounting a charge in R3

stories.title: Tommy Fleetwood in Contention at Hometown Major — Paired With Rahm in R3

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Tommy Fleetwood is 4-under at The Open.

The course? Royal Birkdale — 6 miles from his hometown Southport.

His father is on the bag. He went to his first Open here as a 7-year-old.

No English player has won The Open since Nick Faldo in 1992.

TODAY. 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 #TheOpen #Fleetwood

article.headline: Tommy Fleetwood Is in Contention at His Childhood Major — With His Father on the Bag

research.facts:
Tommy Fleetwood Hometown Hero
**Sources:** CBS Sports (Fleetwood at stake), NBC Sports (R3 tee times)
**Verified facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Fleetwood is from Southport, which is adjacent to Royal Birkdale's location
- He attended his first Open at Birkdale as a 7-year-old in 1998 with his father
- His father is caddying for him this week
- No English player has won The Open Championship since Nick Faldo in 1992
- Fleetwood is at -4 after 36 holes
- He is paired with Jon Rahm in R3
- He is still seeking his first major title
- Jon Rahm (LIV, Legion XIII) shot 67 in R2, also at -4

stories.title: Scheffler Trails by 6 After 36 Holes — Missed 7 Birdie Putts Inside 15 Feet in R2

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Scottie Scheffler hit 13/14 fairways in R1 at Birkdale.

In R2 he missed 7 birdie putts inside 15 feet. Four of them inside 10 feet.

He's -4, 6 back of Herbert. Elite ball-striking. Problem flat stick.

Can he shoot a 63 today? #TheOpen #Scheffler #BritishOpen

article.headline: Scheffler Is Hitting Fairways at a Historic Rate — and Missing Birdie Putts at a Painful One

research.facts:
Scheffler Trails at -4
**Sources:** PGA TOUR (Scheffler R1 recap, R2 putting story)
**Verified facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Scheffler shot 68 in R1 and 68 in R2 (confirmed from PGA TOUR sources)
- After 36 holes: -4, 6 shots behind Herbert
- He hit 13/14 fairways in R1 — best fairway accuracy in the field
- In R2, he missed 7 birdie putts from 15 feet or closer; 4 of those were inside 10 feet
- He is defending Champion Golfer of the Year and World No. 1

stories.title: McIlroy 9 Back But Alive — Made Cut at -1, Needs Heroic Weekend for 7th Major Record

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
McIlroy made the cut at -1. He's 9 back. Long shot — but alive.

Jordan Spieth (2017 Birkdale champion) missed the cut — quad bogey on 17 ended it.

The field is thinning. Moving day is here.

Can Rory hunt down a 7th major? 🤔 #TheOpen #McIlroy #BritishOpen

article.headline: McIlroy Makes the Cut, Spieth Misses — The Open Field Thins for Moving Day

research.facts:
McIlroy Makes Cut, Spieth Misses
**Sources:** ESPN (McIlroy makes cut), CBS Sports (McIlroy R3 lookahead), Bleacher Report (cut list)
**Verified facts (HIGH confidence):**
- McIlroy finished R1 badly (specific R1 score not confirmed in sources but described as "seven shots off the lead" and "flirting with a missed cut")
- McIlroy shot 67 in R2 (-3) to make the cut
- After 36 holes: McIlroy at -1
- He teed off with Jacob Bridgeman early in R3
- He acknowledged "needing a special day" to contend
- He is 9 strokes behind Herbert
- McIlroy is chasing his 7th major title (which would break the European record held jointly with Nick Faldo)
- He won the 2025 and 2026 Masters in back-to-back years
- Jordan Spieth (2017 Birkdale champion) missed the cut with a quadruple bogey on hole 17 in R2
- Other notable missed cuts: Cameron Smith (LIV), Matt Fitzpatrick, Justin Rose, Wyndham Clark, Brian Harman (2023 champion), Padraig Harrington
